warnings: mode=adiabatic: each oxidation state was geometry-optimized separately and the potential uses ΔE between the relaxed geometries (includes reorganization). It does NOT include ZPE/thermal/entropy (ΔE used in place of ΔG); use mode=freq for a true ΔG.

Solvation is implicit/continuum only (or gas phase) — no explicit solvent shell and no Born–Haber solvation cycle. Semi-empirical redox potentials are screening-grade (≈±0.3–0.5 V); even DFT here omits the explicit-solvation correction. Treat as a screening estimate.

E°(vs SHE) uses E_abs(SHE) = 4.281 V. The absolute SHE potential is convention-dependent (literature ≈4.28–4.44 V); this is a systematic offset on the reported potential. Override with --e-abs-she to match your reference convention.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal/mol — shifting pKa by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5. If the molecule has rotatable bonds, run conformer-search first and average, or treat this as a screening estimate tied to the input geometry.

prose:
Standard redox potential (E°) for the 0 → −1 couple of nitrobenzene — GFN2-xTB, adiabatic protocol, implicit water (ALPB continuum) in xtb, referenced to the standard hydrogen electrode (SHE).

Method provenance block
• Property: 1-electron adiabatic redox potential, E° vs SHE, units V
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding; no Gaussian basis)
• Geometry provenance: both oxidized (charge 0, multiplicity 1) and reduced (charge −1, multiplicity 2) states were independently geometry-optimized at GFN2-xTB (xtb ASE BFGS), each converged; the potential is ΔE between the two relaxed geometries (adiabatic, no thermochemistry)
• Solvent / environment: implicit water via xtb’s ALPB continuum (no explicit solvent) — gas-phase electronic energies with continuum reaction field
• Charge & multiplicity: oxidized 0/1 (closed-shell singlet), reduced −1/2 (doublet)
• Software: xtb through chemkit redox-potential skill (chemkit backend 2026-06-25 build; xtb v>6, density-dependent corrections active by default)
• Convergence: both optimizations converged; engine marked integrity.status = ok
• Live log: /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/redox-potential-validation/nitrobenzene/20260625-115548_nitrobenzene_redox_potential/redox_20260625-115556.out (written live; contains SCF/geometry traces)

Headline result
E°(nitrobenzene 0/−1, water, vs SHE) = 4.0129061 V

Caveats
Density-functional/SE methods report electronic ΔE; this adiabatic estimate lacks ZPE, thermal, and entropy corrections. Continuum ALPB omits explicit solvation and the systematic offset in the absolute SHE potential (E_abs = 4.281 V) propagates directly into E°. The result is therefore screening-grade (≈±0.3–0.5 V). See warnings above for full engine notes.
